review_text: Fantastic place to stay, quiet, clean toilets, and nice spots to camp, all what you need near the river.
We turned up late with no booking, and they accommodated us without fuss. Great facilities and staff

Disappointed would be an understatement. We had paid for our 4 night powered site through recommendation. 2 days before our arrival a fireman who's van we were hiring informed us on the high risk of the fires 🔥 turning extreamly nasty. We decided to pull the pin putting our safety first. I contacted the park, they informed me there is no risk. I then request to give us 12 months credit?? Nope nope nuddah !!!!! Poor customer service during a mother nature disaster. Bad Karma River view

We left recently after our first visit to Riverview caravan park. Great location, however ongoing/ multiple issues with not having ANY water or hot water. Toilets/ showers were filthy. Pool also dirty; collection of dirt at the bottom of the pool and cloudy.

Excellent van Park.  Spent a week in a cabin during the Bright Hot Rod and Car show. Had an excellent week the cabin was clean and comfortable with everything I required TV, fully appointed kitchen, A/C and excellent shower. The park was great very beautiful place very pretty little river safe for children "did some fishing no luck" excellent BBQ facilities and the proprietors were a joy to deal with very lovely people. Highly recommend you will definitely enjoy your stay.
Nice little caravan park. Good camp kitchens, clean bathrooms. Good access to the river. Some sites are a bit wonky donkey but work able.
This place is rustic, in a great way. The facilities are top notch and always clean. I didn’t use the pool but it was always clean. The staff were very friendly.

OCT-2025
We had a great time, the Cabin was clean, comfortable, spacious and had everything what we need. The ensuite Cabins are very close to river, this park is surrounded with beautiful trees and lots of greenery.
Always a pleasure, was very peaceful, friendly team 🙌
So peaceful. Amenities are spotless. Camp kitchen is great. Would highly recommend.
This was an affordable option close to all the fantastic attractions the region has to offer. The cabin was very comfortable and it was such an easy process to book and stay. Highly recommend 👌
This is a great park,  we come here often.
Fantastic spot with basic amenities so peaceful after being up Mount Buffalo

I arrived here after a four-hour-drive, and it was a sight for sore eyes! I couldn’t have been happier with the quick and smooth check-in, the tidy and well-appointed cabin, and the quiet and beautiful location. The caravan park is thoughtfully organised and diligently well-maintained; I visited in autumn and the park was ablaze with the colourful foliage of deciduous trees.

Admittedly I spent little time here  however as I had a very busy itinerary, but location-wise I couldn’t have picked a better base camp. Five minutes from the spectacular waterfalls and granite boulders of Mt Buffalo National Park, ten minutes to postcard-pretty Bright, fifteen minutes to many of the farm gates and wineries of the Alpine Valleys region. Porepunkah itself is also a lovely area — travelling along the caravan park driveway late at night, I spotted wild deer and wallabies, and also enjoyed standing outside my cabin just to take in the clear and starry night skies. Looking forward to my next visit here already!

Andrea was very helpful. She really did her best to accommodate our needs...move date, move site all done with a smile. Quiet place after 9-10pm (may be I'm lucky, not sure🤷). Toilets were clean, BBQ and kitchen areas were well taken care of. The grass is not very green but, with foot traffic, I don't belive there's a lushy camp site around there in Autumn.
Great camping grounds, Glen and co run a fantastic service, firepits and wood available, ride or walk to Porepunkah pub.
Me and the family had a great time we will definitely be back
Very nice and quite caravan park. River with crystal clear water. New toilet blocks.

The park is super cute and nice. Well taken care off and the staff is amazing.
Only putting 1 star down cause I couldn't find any grass on the grassy campsite haha, bring sturdy pegs!

As always a fabulous couple of weeks staying there during the summer. Sitting in the river, swimming, sitting next to the river under the shady trees, reading...we loved it all. The teens with us loved playing tenning, and the pool too. Toilet blocks always super clean, and the managers so friendly.

I’m going to break this down into pros and cons. Coming from Lake Sambell Caravan Park in Beechworth (which was wonderful), I had high expectations for this place from the reviews and photos online, but unfortunately it did not live up to our expectations.

Pros
* On the river. Kids can take a floaty and float down the river.
* Friendly and helpful staff.
* Nice pool with free tennis rackets and basketballs for the kids to use.
* There was a pizza van on site on the Friday night.
* Good management. Deals with issues straight away.
* Lots of shade (this can also be a negative as listed below).

Cons
* Toilets not always clean.
* There is only a squeegee for the shower floor, which just pushes mud around. Would highly recommend a bucket of water with a mop for cleaning the showers.
* Only 3 showers and 3 toilets in each of the shower blocks. Not enough for the amount of sites.
* Caravan sites are small and close together compared to other parks we’ve been to.
* Only one outdoor dishwashing station that is far from the unpowered camping area (which is where it is really needed). There is one in the camp kitchen, but is regularly in use with other people using the kitchen.
* Photos are outdated - there were no unpowered grass sites as shown on the website. It is all dirt and leaves.
* Lots of dead branches falling off the trees. Dead branches need to be cut down before they fall and cause damage. We had branches falling onto our site and car.
* Only three washing machines. One doesn’t work well (takes around 2 hours to wash) and one is out of order.
* Not many taps to access water.
* There seems to be a lot of family groups staying in this park, which means late night noise can be an issue.

We ended up leaving 2 days early, not through any fault of the park, but due to a group of camping families (all staying next to each other next to us) having obnoxiously loud nightly get togethers, their kids squealing and yelling, and playing music until late into the night (well past the 10pm quiet time).

We spent 8 months travelling Australia, and stayed in many caravan parks, and this was the first place we stayed where late night noise was an issue. Management was made aware and was straight onto it.

All in all I would rate this park a 6/10, and unfortunately we probably won’t be back (which I hate to say because the owners are lovely).
Brilliant park
Nothing special this Camp , toilet not really clean, not enough toilet and shower, kitchen very small and not even proper kitchen, general place not something! Never again
Nice location, good amenities, friendly staff.

We are having an amazing time here @ Riverview! Fantastic holiday park with all the amenities, very clean and well maintained and right on the river which is safe enough for the kids while being fun enough for all. Special shout out to the owner Glen, who not only parked the caravan for me (was a tricky site for a relatively inexperienced tower) but also after having some hiccups with the caravan that I thought/hoped would be a simple fix but wasn’t, he went absolutely above and beyond to help rectify the problems. His kindness and willingness to help when it was well outside the responsibilities of a caravan park owner will not be forgotten and I wouldn’t hesitate to recommend this park to anyone who wants to visit the area. We will definitely be back!!
Very convenient location when visiting Mt Buffalo. The cabin was clean and everything you needed was there.

Third time staying at this caravan park with our van, right beside the river. Can't beat the peace and serenity. Park well maintained and amenities spotless. Andrea was very helpful and accommodating. Will be back for sure 😁

Spent the night in a no powered camp site $35 per night. Well maintained and clean! The grass is green and soft. I arrived after hours so I didn't meet any staff, but they left information for me in an envelope at the office with clear instructions on where I am located.

No campfires are to be lit on the ground (Understandable), and you will need to hire a bin to lite a fire in, which is unavailable to hire after 3pm.

The facilities are clean. Bathrooms have hot showers, toilet paper, mirror and sink.
Kitchen, is undercover but outside. Microwave, fridge, toaster, kettle,TV table/chairs as well as a sink and dish soap.

Pool is decent size and very clean.
Tennis rackets providedfke their tennis court that is caged.

Great place !

Go for a walk along the stream it's beautiful.
Very peaceful tranquil place to camp

Uninspiring, huge park (200+ sites) jammed into area with just dirt/ gravel footing. It reminded me of a concentration camp. Needs a complete overhaul.
We got our money refunded ( less $4.40 for admin!) and left to find Ah Young's campsite up the Buckland valley.
Very clean park, nice and quiet, well kept!

Had the best weekend in this caravan park again.  The owners are the friendliest most easy going people we have ever had the pleasure of dealing with and the park amenities are fantastic.  Thanks Glen and Andrea for another memorable weekend away with family. We love making family memories in your park 💙💜

I was watching my two kids swimming in the pool area for some time before one of the other parents we knew there sent her 3 boys in to swim while I was there. I observed one of the boys bomb into the water and said to him, no bombing, there is a sign on the gate "no bombing." He didn't do it again.

The kids (whom are all 12 and under) all continued to swim for the afternoon  jumping in along the edge, not disrupting other swimmers or being a nuisance to any other swimmers, when Bonnie (whom I have come to learn work's in reception and is the owner Glen's sister in law), screamed, and I do mean aggressively screamed, GET OUT,  GET OUT NOW! She charged through the pool gate, so I asked why and she yelled "because the boy had bombed into the pool". I said I saw him actually just scissor jump in at the edge. She argued with me before walking off and proceeded to yell something to which I was later told by the other mum and another child, that the part I had missed her say was "IF I SEE YOU GET BACK IN THE POOL AGAIN, I WILL DRAG YOU OUT, AND DON'T TAKE ME FOR A FOOL BECAUSE I MEAN IT." The boy she yelled at was left terrified  and wouldn't join in with his friends until we saw her drive out of the park.

The following morning I checked out at the time of 10 am and rebooked thinking it was unlikely I would be staying again, but another mum wanted my cabin if I didn't use that time myself. After I booked with Bonnie who was working at the desk, I said to her that on a separate note, I felt that she acted completely inappropriately at the pool the day before  and that if I see her talk to a child like that again, I would report it. She launched straight back with the same aggressive attempt to intimidate me, stating that she is not there to be abused and that I should go somewhere else if I wasn't happy with the manner she addressed inappropriate behaviour. Once I had asked her to just stop and listen to me, I replied with the fact that I had absolutely no issue with her calling out bad behaviour, as I had done so before when one of the boy's had bombed in, but that the way she had gone about it was totally inappropriate and very aggressive. Instead of apologising or taking on board my concern, which I adressed with her very calmly, she continued her rant and asked if I would like to see the cctv. When I replied yes, she just carried on yelling at me. Again, I confirmed what I had told her and walked out the office. Two other women who were guests there and had been in the office at this time,  had heard her say, as I had gone to step outside, that" NEXT TIME SHE WILL GET THE GLOVES OUT" and asked me if I had heard this. which I hadn't.

Twenty minutes later I recieved a call from Glen, the owner of Riverview Caravan Park. He actually owns Mt Buffallo Caravan Park on the other side of the river as well.

I calmly discussed what had occurred by the pool and also about what had happened with Bonnie. Glen then went onto say that because I hadn't heard it myself that I was an INSINUATING LIAR and that I should have been watching my kid's, and continued to carry on about other kid's bombing into the pool the previous day to this incident  which h is completely irrelevant to what had happened. I replied the same way as I did to his sister in law, to which he was very offended by the fact I had confronted her, that I had no issue with calling out issues with people or children not following rules, but again, it was aggressive and unexceptable. I also said that he was there to run a family caravan park and kids are there to have a nice time. He got VERY frustrated and put me on loud speaker when I repeated what had been said to my back as I had walked out the office, for me to say it to Bonnie, which I did. He then told me he was cancelling my future booking and that I was not welcome at his park, and I was to get my caravan or whatever is was I had and go away,  and then hung up the phone on me.

So take from it what you will. I am not ok with this, and would much rather visit one of the many other beautiful parks in the region.
Nice place to stay in a cabin or a site, along Buckland river very close to bright, a bit run down and ok and clean amenities, friendly customer service.

This was our first time here and the location was amazing! Park is super clean  and we were lucky enough to have river front camp.
Only down side was the amenities. We felt this was not adequate enough to cater for the amount of sites, often waiting a while to use the toilet n shower.
What an awesome place. Love it so much. Still the best place to stay.
My family goes here every year. Recently the owners have added a pool, and updated a few things. Definitely recommend, bring lots of warm clothes in winter, and lots of sunscreen in summer.

If I could give it them zero stars I would. They wouldn't let us reschedule, wouldn't give a refund when we couldn't come that day. So enjoy my donation of $38 for a bloody unpowered site. If you don't want to donate and get nothing back then maybe think twice about having to reschedule! 👍👍👍👍

We had a powered site facing the river
Awesome position. We enjoyed our recent stay and would recommend it to others. The staff were very helpful and friendly, particularly the bubbly lady in the office
terrible showers, bugs everywhere in the bathrooms, water pressure was well below average and they randomly turn off the water in the day.
Beautiful part of the world 🌎

Absolutely fabulous 3 night stay.  We were upgraded to a bigger cabin so that we could be closer to our family in caravans.  Great amenities.  Comfy queen bed. Basic necessities in the bathroom with a fully equipped kitchen in the cabin.  Hosts are brilliant. Would definitely come again.

Caravan park is in a quiet location and has modern facilities and it was in perfect place for Mt Buffulo. It allows for quick access to all walks and lookouts in national park.  Great location and staff extremely friendly.

This place is highly recommended by me .
Cabins are very well equipped.
Very clean
All the kitchen stuff supplied.
Bedding and towels are very high quality.
Very caring and supportive staff.
We are already booking for next year.
Prices are very good.
